subject: coming home from the war
lyrics:
[Verse]
The bus dropped me at the county line
Dust on my boots and a duffel of time
Mama's porch light still burns the same
Though the dog at the gate forgot my name

[Verse]
The fields are taller than I recall
The church still leans by the cattle stall
I counted these roads on a desert night
Now they're rolling under my own headlights

[Chorus]
I'm coming home, coming home
Where the river knows my name
I left a boy with something to prove
I'm back a man just glad I came
Carry me down that old dirt road
Lay my rifle by the door
I'm coming home, coming home
I don't have to march no more

[Verse]
She meets me halfway up the lane
Wearing the dress from the day of the train
We don't say much, we just hold on
To all the years that the war took gone

[Bridge]
There's a flag they folded for the man next door
And I carry his name like a stone in my core
So I'll plant a tree and I'll work this land
For every soldier who couldn't stand

[Chorus]
I'm coming home, coming home
Where the river knows my name
I left a boy with something to prove
I'm back a man just glad I came

subject: an elder teaching the tide
lyrics:
[Verse]
Grandfather walks where the warm water breaks
Barefoot and slow as the morning awakes
He whispers a count to the foam at his knees
Teaching the ocean to move with the breeze

[Verse]
Soft on the sand, soft on the bay
He says little waves, you've all day to play
Don't rush to the shore, don't crash on the stone
Come in like a song that the moon has known

[Chorus]
Go gentle, go gentle, the old man sings
In and out on the silver strings
The tide leans close like a child that learns
And carries his patience the way it returns

[Verse]
He was taught by his mother who sat in this place
Who hummed the same lullaby into the space
Now he hands it to me with a nod and a smile
Stay with the rhythm, just stay for a while

[Bridge]
When he is gone I will stand in the swell
And count out the secret he taught me so well
The sea never hurries, the sea understands
It was always just learning to hold someone's hands

[Chorus]
Go gentle, go gentle, the old man sings
In and out on the silver strings
The tide leans close like a child that learns
And carries his patience the way it returns

subject: driving past your old apartment at night
lyrics:
[Verse]
Third floor window, the curtains are new
Somebody's living the life we outgrew
I slow at the corner, I don't even stop
Just let the engine hum down to a drop

[Verse]
The streetlight still flickers the way that it did
The night that we argued, the night that we hid
From everything coming, from everything gone
I keep my foot light and I keep rolling on

[Chorus]
And the city goes quiet, the city goes wide
I'm a ghost in a car on the other side
Of a window that glows with a stranger's warm light
Driving past who we were tonight

[Verse]
No words could hold all the weight of this street
So the guitars just rise where the silence and memory meet
A swell and a crash and a slow letting go
Of a door I no longer can know

[Bridge]
Let it build, let it break, let it ring out and fade
Every mile is a vow that the distance has made
I won't turn around, I won't circle the block
I'll let the long highway unlock

[Chorus]
And the city goes quiet, the city goes wide
I'm a ghost in a car on the other side
Of a window that glows with a stranger's warm light
Driving past who we were tonight

subject: a poet who won't sell out
lyrics:
[Verse]
They offered her a billboard and a brand new name
A jingle for a soda, a shot at the fame
She folded up the contract into a paper bird
Said keep your shiny money, I'm staying with my word

[Pre-Chorus]
They said honey, who's gonna hear you
In a coffee shop at half past nine
She just smiled and tuned her old guitar
And said the truth still rhymes

[Chorus]
She won't sell out, she won't sell out
The pen's still hers and the page is loud
You can have the charts, you can have the crowd
She'd rather mean it than be allowed
She won't sell out, no, she won't sell out

[Verse]
She writes about the city and the rent that's too high
The lovers on the rooftop and the planes in the sky
No committee gets to edit how the heartbreak feels
No focus group decides what's real

[Bridge]
Maybe she stays small, maybe she stays poor
But the kid in the back row knows what she's for
Every honest line is a window, not a wall
And that's worth more than all of it, after all

[Chorus]
She won't sell out, she won't sell out
The pen's still hers and the page is loud
You can have the charts, you can have the crowd
She'd rather mean it than be allowed

subject: a tailor who dresses the whole block
lyrics:
[Verse]
Old Mister Romano on the corner of Vine
Has a needle that dances and a measuring line
From the banker to the busboy, from the bride to the beau
They all come knocking when they've got somewhere to go

[Chorus]
He'll cut you a collar, he'll press you a crease
He'll have you looking sharp as the chief of police
Snap those suspenders, give your hat a little knock
Mister Romano dresses the whole block

[Verse]
The widow on Friday gets her hemline just right
The trumpet man Saturday is sharp for the night
A stitch and a wink and a do-wah-dee-doo
He sends 'em out swinging in something brand new

[Verse]
He never takes credit, he never takes much
Just coffee and gossip and the pride of the touch
When the neighborhood marches in a wedding parade
Every thread on the avenue is something he made

[Bridge]
So here's to the hands that the city forgets
The quiet little maestro of cuffs and lapels
When they bury him someday in his very best gray
The whole block will be dapper as they wave him away

[Chorus]
He'll cut you a collar, he'll press you a crease
He'll have you looking sharp as the chief of police
Snap those suspenders, give your hat a little knock
Mister Romano dresses the whole block

subject: a city built on a sleeping giant
lyrics:
[Verse]
They laid the cobblestones on a slumbering chest
Built the cathedral right over his vest
The trolleys all rattle, the dancers all spin
On the back of a giant who's tucked his chin in

[Chorus]
So tiptoe, baby, tiptoe and sway
Keep the big fella dreaming till the end of the day
A little soft shoe, keep it light on the ground
We live on a giant, so don't make a sound

[Verse]
The mayor gives speeches on the rise of his ribs
The lovers go strolling down the lines of his fibs
When he sighs in his sleep, why the church bells will chime
And we call it an earthquake and we dance right on time

[Verse]
The bakers, the bankers, the band on the square
All built their whole lives on a breath of his air
Nobody dares ask what becomes of it all
The morning he wakes and decides to stand tall

[Bridge]
So play it real gentle, play it real sweet
Let the lullaby drift down to his feet
For a city's just borrowed from something asleep
And the rent comes due when the dreamers can't keep

[Chorus]
So tiptoe, baby, tiptoe and sway
Keep the big fella dreaming till the end of the day
A little soft shoe, keep it light on the ground
We live on a giant, so don't make a sound

subject: the last night of a festival
lyrics:
[Verse]
The lanterns are burning their final red hour
The square is still trembling with heel and with flower
One more guitar bleeds against the white wall
The last night is longest, the last night is all

[Chorus]
Baila, baila, before the dawn comes
Before they sweep up the petals and silence the drums
One more turn of the wrist, one more cry to the sky
The festival's ending and we don't say goodbye

[Verse]
A grandmother claps with her eyes shut and wet
For every farewell that the years won't forget
The boy with the roses runs out of his roses
And still the night opens what morning will close

[Verse]
The wine has gone warm and the dancers won't stop
We stamp out our sorrow till the last lantern drops
The street will be empty when the sun finds the stone
But tonight we are burning, tonight we're not alone

[Bridge]
Ay, the smoke and the strings and the salt on the air
Ay, the love that we spend like we've plenty to spare
For a year we will wait with this fire in our chest
So give me the last night, I'll give you the rest

[Chorus]
Baila, baila, before the dawn comes
Before they sweep up the petals and silence the drums
One more turn of the wrist, one more cry to the sky
The festival's ending and we don't say goodbye

subject: the last day of summer before college
lyrics:
[Verse]
We parked by the water where the willows hang low
With a cooler half empty and nowhere to go
Your bags are all packed in your daddy's old truck
And tomorrow we're trading these long days for luck

[Verse]
We carved our initials in the rail of the dock
Watched the sun melt to gold on the face of the clock
You're heading out east and I'm staying behind
With a summer of memories crowding my mind

[Chorus]
So let the radio play one more time
Let the fireflies rise on the county line
Hold my hand till the morning comes
This is the last day of being young
The last day of summer, the last of the sun

[Verse]
We made a hundred promises we might never keep
Drove the back roads slow just to make the night deep
You said don't be sad, this is how it should be
But your eyes when you said it weren't lying to me

[Bridge]
We'll be different people by the cold of December
With new towns and faces we'll learn to remember
But this dock and this water and this dust in the air
Will be waiting whenever we need it back there

[Chorus]
So let the radio play one more time
Let the fireflies rise on the county line
Hold my hand till the morning comes
This is the last day of being young

subject: leaving your small town for good
lyrics:
[Verse]
Got one suitcase and a midnight train
The diner's still lit through the window pane
I memorized the streets like the back of a song
Now I'm humming goodbye to where I belong

[Verse]
The steeple, the soda shop, the boy on the stoop
The same six faces in the same little loop
They'll wave from the platform, they'll wish me the moon
But they'll be home for supper and forget me by June

[Chorus]
So long, little town, I'm taking the line
Where the saxophone bends and the city lights shine
I loved you, I did, but I outgrew your size
Now I'm chasing the smoke where the tall buildings rise

[Verse]
I'll miss the slow river, the slow Sunday bell
The slow way they say all the stories they tell
But something in me runs a faster blue beat
And it won't be denied by a quiet old street

[Bridge]
Maybe I'll come back when my hair's going gray
With a trunk full of cities and stories to play
Or maybe this whistle is the last that they hear
Of the kid who got restless and just disappeared

[Chorus]
So long, little town, I'm taking the line
Where the saxophone bends and the city lights shine
I loved you, I did, but I outgrew your size
Now I'm chasing the smoke where the tall buildings rise

subject: driving past your old apartment at night
lyrics:
[Verse]
Tinted windows, low beams on the block
Roll past the building where I lost the plot
3 a.m., the city sound a hum
That fourth floor where I used to come from
New curtains hanging where my whole world stayed
New name on the buzzer, that's the price I paid
Grip on the wheel, I ain't even slow it down
Ghost in the leather, riding silent through the town

[Chorus]
Drive past, don't stop, don't look (don't look)
Every street a page from a closed-up book
I made it out, made it out, I'm gone (I'm gone)
But my heartbeat skip when I hit that lawn
Drive past, don't stop, let it blur
That's the old me living back with her

[Verse]
Same potholes, same flicker on the sign
Same cracked steps where I wasted my time
Now the chains heavy and the whip clean
But I'm seventeen again at that scene
Memory loud even when the bass low
Pull my hood up, tell the driver let's go
Can't go home cause home don't exist
Just a window and a wave and a fist

[Bridge]
I don't miss it, I don't miss it (I swear)
Then why I keep circling back over there
One more loop and I'm done with the past
Green light, foot down, drive fast

[Chorus]
Drive past, don't stop, don't look (don't look)
Every street a page from a closed-up book
I made it out, made it out, I'm gone (I'm gone)
But my heartbeat skip when I hit that lawn

subject: taping up the last box when you move out
lyrics:
[Verse]
Last roll of the tape going screech 'cross the seam
Empty little room where I lived out a dream
Echo in the corner where the sofa used to be
Little bit of dust and a whole lotta me

[Chorus]
Seal it up now, seal it up slow
Everything I carried, everything I know
Goodbye little doorway, goodbye little wall
One more box and I'm carrying it all
Irie, irie, let the past roll on
One more piece of tape and I'm gone

[Verse]
Marker on the cardboard say kitchen and books
But it can't write down how the morning light looks
Coming through that window on a slow Sunday rise
Some things you can't pack, you just keep in your eyes

[Verse]
Keys on the counter and a note on the floor
Thank you little flat, I won't need you no more
But you held me together when the rent was so steep
So I leave you my echo for the next one to keep

[Bridge]
Every ending, mon, is a riddim, not a stop
Just the bassline moving while the old verse drop
Lift it on my shoulder, feel it light as a song
Greet the open road where I'm rolling along

[Chorus]
Seal it up now, seal it up slow
Everything I carried, everything I know
Goodbye little doorway, goodbye little wall
One more box and I'm carrying it all

subject: a museum guard and a painting
lyrics:
[Verse]
Every night at the gallery when the patrons go home
There's a guard named McKinley walking marble alone
But he stops at a portrait of a girl in blue lace
And he tips his old cap to her sweet painted face

[Chorus]
Good evening, mademoiselle, fancy meeting you here
Same spot on the wall for a hundred-odd years
I'll watch over you, doll, till the morning bells chime
Just you and me swinging in our overtime

[Verse]
He tells her his troubles, he tells her his day
The pension, the weather, the rent he can't pay
And her smile never changes, but he swears that it might
Grow a fraction warmer in the low gallery light

[Verse]
The curators don't know and the cameras don't care
That a lonely old fella found a love painted there
When the brass section's done and the city's gone still
He'll waltz down the hallway and he always will

[Bridge]
They can sell her tomorrow to a man in Marseille
They can crate up his sweetheart and ship her away
But he'll keep the last dance like a card up his sleeve
For the girl in blue lace who could never quite leave

[Chorus]
Good evening, mademoiselle, fancy meeting you here
Same spot on the wall for a hundred-odd years
I'll watch over you, doll, till the morning bells chime
Just you and me swinging in our overtime

subject: taping up the last box when you move out
lyrics:
[Verse]
The pack tape cries when I pull it across
Sealing up the years like I'm sealing up a loss
Floorboards remember every barefoot night
Now the room's just an outline in the afternoon light

[Chorus]
Ooh, last box, baby, last box
Holding all the love that this old place holds
Set it by the door, let the new air in
Funny how an ending feels like skin
Last box, oh, last box

[Verse]
There's a ring on the counter from a glass of red wine
A story in the carpet that was yours and was mine
I fold up the silence and I label it home
Then I carry the quiet down the hall on my own

[Verse]
I could play it so smooth, act like nothing's the same
But the walls keep humming back my own name
Groove of the radiator, hum of the street
Everything I'm leaving's got a heartbeat

[Bridge]
So I'll breathe in this empty, let it settle in my chest
Give thanks for the shelter, lay the rest down to rest
Love don't stay in plaster, love don't stay in the floor
It rides on out with me through the door

[Chorus]
Ooh, last box, baby, last box
Holding all the love that this old place holds
Set it by the door, let the new air in
Funny how an ending feels like skin

subject: the northern lights
lyrics:
[Verse]
Up on the cold hill where the breath turns to smoke
The sky start to shimmer like the heavens awoke
Green ribbon dancing, gold and violet too
Mama said the spirits come to dance for you

[Chorus]
Watch the lights, watch the lights, how they sway in the cold
Like a riddim from the angels in the silver and gold
No sound but the wonder and the stars up above
The northern sky moving to a slow song of love

[Verse]
We come from the islands where the sun never sleep
Now we stand in the white land where the snow lies deep
But the same heart a beat under different sky
And it lift like the colors when they ripple on high

[Verse]
The fire burning low and the children all stare
At the curtains of glory in the frozen night air
No king got a palace and no rich got a crown
Like the kingdom of color coming softly down

[Bridge]
Jah paint with no brush, Jah sing with no string
Just the hush of the cosmos and the wonder it bring
So we hold one another and we watch the sky burn
Till the dawn take it back and the morning return

[Chorus]
Watch the lights, watch the lights, how they sway in the cold
Like a riddim from the angels in the silver and gold
No sound but the wonder and the stars up above
The northern sky moving to a slow song of love

subject: studying alone at two in the morning
lyrics:
[Verse]
Desk lamp humming and the coffee gone cold
Highlighter bleeding through the page I don't hold
The dorm's gone silent, even the trains
And it's just me and the dark and these caffeinated brains

[Verse]
The clock says two and the chapter says nine
The words start swimming in a horizontal line
Somewhere somebody's asleep in a bed
And I'm underlining theorems in my head

[Chorus]
And I'm wide awake, I'm wide awake
With the whole world dreaming and a test to take
Just the buzz of the light and the turn of the page
A little kingdom of one at this hour and age
Wide awake, oh, wide awake

[Verse]
I could quit and just sleep, let it all fall apart
But there's something half-holy in this thing I start
The quiet, the focus, the war that I wage
With my own tired eyes and a blank notebook page

[Bridge]
Nobody sees it, nobody knows
How the future gets built in these dim little shows
When the morning comes gold over the quad
I'll have nothing to show but a passing nod

[Chorus]
And I'm wide awake, I'm wide awake
With the whole world dreaming and a test to take
Just the buzz of the light and the turn of the page
A little kingdom of one at this hour and age
